title: clematis henryi subsp. morii
intro: Clematis henryi subsp. morii, also known as Clematis morii, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Clematis genus, native to China and Southeast Asia. It is a climbing perennial vine with showy, fragrant flowers that are often used in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which may be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throat, and skin infections. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and boost the immune system.
